title: Downward Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasana)
description: Adho Mukha Svanasana, also known as Downward Dog. Improves flexibility, strengthens arms and legs, relieves back pain, energizes the body.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents surrender and humility. Energizes the body and calms the mind.
instructions:
1. Start on hands and knees.
2. Spread fingers wide, tuck toes, lift hips up and back.
3. Keep spine straight, heels pressing toward the floor.
4. Hold for 5-10 breaths.
contraindications: carpal tunnel syndrome, severe hypertension, pregnancy (consult doctor).
benefits: calms the mind and relieves stress
precautions: keep knees slightly bent if hamstrings are tight
tips: press evenly through palms and fingers
engage the core to protect the lower back

title: Tree Pose (Vrikshasana)
description: Vrikshasana, also called Tree Pose. Improves balance, strengthens legs, opens hips, enhances concentration.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Symbolizes rootedness and stability. Promotes grounding and focus.
instructions:
1. Stand tall with feet together.
2. Shift weight onto left foot, place right foot on inner left thigh.
3. Bring palms together at heart or overhead.
4. Hold for 5-10 breaths, then switch sides.
contraindications: knee or ankle injury, balance issues (use support).
precautions: avoid pressing foot directly on the knee
use a wall for support if balance is difficult
tips: gaze at a fixed point for stability

title: Mountain Pose (Tadasana)
description: Tadasana, also called Mountain Pose. Foundation for all standing poses. Improves posture, strengthens legs, and enhances body awareness.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents stability and grounding. Balances energy and calms the mind.
instructions:
1. Stand with feet together, arms at sides.
2. Distribute weight evenly across both feet.
3. Engage thigh muscles, lift chest, and lengthen spine.
4. Inhale, raise arms overhead, palms facing each other.
5. Hold for 5-10 breaths.
contraindications: severe low back pain or recent surgery (consult doctor).
tips: distribute weight evenly on both feet

title: Warrior I (Virabhadrasana I)
description: Virabhadrasana I, or Warrior I. Strengthens legs, stretches hips, opens chest, improves focus and stamina.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents courage and inner strength. Energizes the body and builds determination.
instructions:
1. Step one foot back, front knee bent, back leg straight.
2. Keep hips facing forward.
3. Raise arms overhead, palms facing each other.
4. Hold for 5-10 breaths, then switch sides.
contraindications: knee or hip injury, high blood pressure (consult doctor).
precautions: keep front knee aligned over ankle

title: Warrior II (Virabhadrasana II)
description: Virabhadrasana II, or Warrior II. Strengthens legs, opens hips, stretches shoulders, improves stamina and focus.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents courage, determination, and readiness. Energizes body while calming the mind.
instructions:
1. Step one foot back, front knee bent, back leg straight.
2. Turn torso to the side, arms extended parallel to floor.
3. Gaze over front hand.
4. Hold for 5-10 breaths, then switch sides.
contraindications: knee or hip injury, dizziness (consult doctor).

title: Warrior III (Virabhadrasana III)
description: Virabhadrasana III, or Warrior III, is a balancing pose that strengthens legs, core, and back while improving concentration and stability.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents focus, determination, and forward movement. Energizes body and mind.
instructions:
1. Start in Warrior I position.
2. Shift weight onto front leg, lift back leg parallel to the floor.
3. Lean torso forward, arms extended overhead or forward.
4. Keep body in one straight line from fingertips to lifted foot.
5. Hold for 5-10 breaths, then switch sides.
contraindications: knee, ankle, or lower back injuries, balance issues (use support).
precautions: keep hips square to the floor
use wall for support if balance is difficult
tips: reach through both arms and lifted leg

title: Cobra Pose (Bhujangasana)
description: Bhujangasana, or Cobra Pose, is a gentle backbend that strengthens the spine, stretches the chest, and stimulates abdominal organs.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents awakening and expansion. Opens heart and energizes the body.
instructions:
1. Lie face down, legs together, hands under shoulders.
2. Press tops of feet and thighs into floor.
3. Inhale, lift chest while keeping elbows slightly bent.
4. Roll shoulders back and keep neck neutral.
5. Hold for 5-10 breaths.
contraindications: recent abdominal surgery, severe back injury, pregnancy (consult doctor).
tips: press evenly through pelvis and feet

title: Sphinx Pose (Sphinx Asana)
description: Sphinx Pose is a gentle backbend that strengthens the spine, opens the chest, and stimulates the abdominal organs while being very beginner-friendly.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents awakening and alertness. Energizes the body and calms the mind.
instructions:
1. Lie on stomach, legs together, elbows under shoulders, forearms on the floor.
2. Press forearms into the floor, lift chest and head.
3. Draw shoulders back and down, keep neck neutral.
4. Hold for 5-10 breaths.

title: Spinal Twist (Merudanda Twist / Parivartanasana)
description: Parivartanasana, or Spinal Twist, is a seated or supine twist that stretches the spine, massages internal organs, and improves digestion.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents release and detoxification. Calms the nervous system and balances energy.
instructions:
1. Sit or lie on your back.
2. Cross one leg over the other or bend knees toward chest if supine.
3. Twist spine gently to one side, shoulders grounded.
4. Hold for 5-10 breaths, then switch sides.
contraindications: spinal injury, severe back pain, pregnancy (consult doctor).
precautions: keep spine long and shoulders grounded
tips: inhale to lengthen spine, exhale to twist gently
use a cushion under hips if seated

title: Camel Pose (Ustrasana)
description: Ustrasana, or Camel Pose, is a deep backbend that stretches the front body, opens the chest, and strengthens the back muscles.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents openness and courage. Energizes the body and stimulates the heart chakra.
instructions:
1. Kneel on floor, knees hip-width apart.
2. Place hands on lower back or heels.
3. Press hips forward, lift chest, and gently arch back.
4. Keep neck neutral or drop head back slightly.
5. Hold for 5-10 breaths.
contraindications: neck or back injury, pregnancy (consult doctor).
benefits: stretches chest, abdomen, and hip flexors

title: Cow Pose (Gomukha / Cow Pose)
description: Cow Pose is a gentle backbend performed with Cat Pose to mobilize the spine, improve posture, and relieve tension in the back.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents openness and flexibility. Energizes the spine and balances body and mind.
instructions:
1. Start on hands and knees, wrists under shoulders, knees under hips.
2. Inhale, lift chest and tailbone, drop belly toward floor (Cow).
3. Exhale, round spine, tuck chin to chest (Cat) – often done as a flowing sequence.
4. Repeat 5-10 cycles.
contraindications: wrist, neck, or back injury (consult doctor).

title: Crescent Lunge (Ardha Chandra Lunge)
description: Ardha Chandra Lunge is a high lunge that strengthens legs, stretches hip flexors, and improves balance and stability.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents forward momentum and determination. Energizes the lower body and builds focus.
instructions:
1. Step one foot forward into a lunge, back leg straight.
2. Align front knee over ankle, hips facing forward.
3. Inhale, raise arms overhead, palms facing each other.
4. Hold for 5-10 breaths, then switch sides.
contraindications: knee or hip injury, balance issues (use support).

title: Upward Salute (Urdhva Hastasana)
description: Urdhva Hastasana, or Upward Salute, is a standing stretch that lengthens the spine, opens the chest, and improves posture and focus.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents reaching upward and openness. Energizes the body and calms the mind.
instructions:
1. Stand tall in Mountain Pose (Tadasana).
2. Inhale, raise arms overhead, palms facing each other.
3. Reach fingertips toward the sky, lengthening the spine.
4. Hold for 5-10 breaths.
contraindications: severe shoulder or back injury, dizziness (consult doctor).

title: Eagle Pose (Garudasana)
description: Garudasana, or Eagle Pose, is a balancing posture that strengthens legs and ankles, stretches shoulders and upper back, and improves focus.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents focus, precision, and the ability to rise above. Energizes the body and improves concentration.
instructions:
1. Stand in Mountain Pose.
2. Bend knees slightly, lift right leg, cross it over left thigh, hook foot behind left calf.
3. Cross arms at elbows, palms together.
4. Hold for 5-10 breaths, then switch sides.
contraindications: knee, ankle, or shoulder injuries, balance issues (use support).
precautions: avoid pressing foot directly on the knee
use a wall for support if balance is difficult
tips: gaze at a fixed point for stability

title: Lord of the Dance Pose (Natarajasana)
description: Natarajasana, or Lord of the Dance Pose, is a graceful balancing backbend that strengthens legs, stretches chest and shoulders, and improves focus and poise.
symbolismAndEnergetics: Represents balance, grace, and cosmic dance. Energizes body and mind while improving focus.
instructions:
1. Stand in Mountain Pose.
2. Shift weight onto left leg, bend right knee, reach back and hold right foot.
3. Inhale, lift left arm forward or overhead, chest lifted.
4. Lean forward slightly, keeping balance.
5. Hold for 5-10 breaths, then switch sides.
contraindications: knee, ankle, shoulder, or back injury, balance issues (use support).
precautions: keep standing leg firm and knee slightly soft
use wall for support if balance is difficult
tips: reach through lifted arm and extended leg
